mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output, which lifts aggregate corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or average forward returns in the top INDPRO quintile are not higher than the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 8.5% indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held near zero while consumer price inflation accelerates, negative real interest rates erode fixed-income competitiveness and heighten future monetary tightening fears, jointly depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflationary pressure
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce a negative coefficient significant at the 5% level in a 24-month rolling regression, or 20-day returns during joint high-inflation low-rate periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries with policy rates at the zero lower bound, quarterly real GDP growth surprises serve as the dominant equity driver because discount rate effects are muted; higher GDPC1 growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with zero lower bound rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-median GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2% signals abrupt weakness in consumer demand that compresses corporate top-line growth, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a greater than 2% monthly drop in RSAFS are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean 5-day return during such triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes where joblessness exceeds 8%, positive surprises in nonfarm payrolls indicate nascent labor market repair that reduces recession risk and drives outsized positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly higher when UNRATE exceeds 8% than when UNRATE is at or below 8% (interaction t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC in this regime falls below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above 600 thousand units signals a durable turnaround in residential construction activity that ripples through employment and materials demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of trading days when HOUST exceeds 600, or the mean 5-day return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25% suppresses the risk-free discount rate and pushes investors out the risk curve, creating persistent upward pressure on equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly higher than during periods with FEDFUNDS above 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window.
